pr.title: mp: Match mpLib_80053ECC_Floor and update mpisland.h signatures
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Attempted to match `mpLib_80053ECC_Floor` in the map/collision library by rewriting its predecessor-floor traversal loop into an explicit goto/label structure for codegen. The PR body also claims `mpisland.h` signatures were updated from `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` to typed signatures, but the provided changed-file metadata and diff excerpt only show edits to `src/melee/mp/mplib.c`. The PR was closed unmerged after the author noted recently merged changes affected the match percentage and that the work needed rework.

key_files.reason: Only changed file in the provided PR metadata; contains the rewrite of `mpLib_80053ECC_Floor` from a structured `while (true)` loop to explicit labels/gotos.
Mentioned in the PR title/body as having signature updates from placeholders to proper types, but not present in the provided changed-files list or diff excerpt, so this claim is weakly evidenced.

what_changed: `mpLib_80053ECC_Floor(int line_id, Vec* vec)` was rewritten to use `goto loop_entry`, `loop_start`, and `loop_entry` labels instead of a `while (true)` loop with `break`.
The traversal still follows `groundCollLine[line_id].x0->prev_id0` while the previous line exists and has `CollLine_Floor` set.
Local declarations were adjusted: `new_id` was moved out of the loop and `shifted` was introduced before `CollVtx* temp_r3`.
The PR body says `mpisland.h` function signatures were updated from `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` placeholders to proper types, but that header change is not visible in the supplied diff excerpt.
smart_moves: Used explicit labels and gotos to mimic compiler-emitted loop shape rather than preserving nicer C control flow, a common matching tactic when structured loops produce different branch layout.
Pulled `new_id` into outer scope so it can participate in the label-based loop without redeclaration at the loop entry point.
Kept existing safety/debug checks via `LINEID_CHECK(4448, line_id)` before traversal and `LINEID_CHECK(4453, line_id)` after traversal, preserving the function’s validation structure.
Documented the intended validation: build passes with `ninja` and `mpLib_80053ECC_Floor` matched 100% on decomp.me at the time of submission.
decomp_lessons: For matching loops that walk collision adjacency data, explicit goto-based loop reconstruction may be required when a natural `while` loop does not reproduce the target branch layout.
Control-flow matching can be fragile across nearby merges; the author later reported that recently merged changes affected the function’s match percentage.
When claiming header/signature cleanup, make sure the changed-files list and diff actually include the relevant header, or reviewers/future agents cannot verify the type work from the PR slice.
Keep line-ID validation macros in their original relative positions when refactoring matching code around collision line traversal.
naming_conventions: `mpLib_80053ECC_Floor` follows the project’s decomp naming pattern of subsystem prefix `mpLib`, address-like identifier, and semantic suffix `_Floor`.
`mpLib_80053DA4_Floor` appears adjacent in the diff context and uses the same `_Floor` suffix convention.
Collision structures and constants shown include `CollVtx`, `groundCollLine`, `CollLine_Floor`, and `LINEID_CHECK`.
Placeholder signature names mentioned in the PR body use `UNK_RET` and `UNK_PARAMS`, which should be replaced with concrete return and parameter types once known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace a structured `while (true)`/`break` loop with `goto loop_entry` and `goto loop_start` to force a specific loop entry and backedge shape.
Declare loop-carried variables such as `new_id` before labels to avoid C scoping that prevents goto-based reconstruction.
Introduce temporaries such as `shifted = line_id << 3` when needed to influence register allocation or address computation, though the provided excerpt does not show how `shifted` is later used.
Preserve exact condition ordering: first check `new_id != -1`, then test `groundCollLine[new_id].flags & CollLine_Floor` before updating `line_id`.

review_feedback.feedback: The author said they needed to rework the PR because recently merged changes affected its match percentage and planned to switch it to draft.
review_feedback.impact: Explains why the PR was closed unmerged and highlights that the submitted match was not stable against current master.

review_feedback.feedback: No formal review comments or reviews were present in the provided slice.
review_feedback.impact: There is no reviewer guidance beyond the author's own note.

follow_up_queries: Find the later PR or commit that successfully matched `mpLib_80053ECC_Floor` after the merged changes affected this attempt.
Inspect whether `mpisland.h` signature updates were moved to another PR, since they are mentioned here but not visible in the supplied changed-files metadata.
Compare current `src/melee/mp/mplib.c` implementation of `mpLib_80053ECC_Floor` against this goto-based attempt to see which matching tactic survived.
Search for other `mpLib_*_Floor` functions using `groundCollLine[line_id].x0->prev_id0` to reuse the traversal pattern.
